seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with Agno + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with Agno + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with Agno enables developers to create powerful, multi-modal agents that understand repositories, execute code in sandboxes, and iteratively debug issues. However, these agents face risks like generating insecure code, leaking credentials, or failing structured validation during complex coding tasks. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify code correctness, security, and compliance before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with Agno
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Agno AI Coding Assistant architecture starts with a code-aware LLM agent that ingests repository context via tools like GitHub loaders and file parsers. It maintains session memory for iterative debugging sessions and uses sandboxed execution environments to test generated code. Test validation runs automatically, with the agent iterating based on failures until passing all checks.

Multi-modal capabilities allow image-based code screenshots or diagram analysis, while structured outputs ensure generated code follows specific schemas. Team coordination enables multiple specialized agents (e.g., security reviewer, test writer) to collaborate on complex tasks. The agent.run() method orchestrates the full workflow from problem statement to verified solution.

Vex integration wraps critical execution points to verify code safety, dependency integrity, and compliance before any code leaves the sandbox.

risks.name: Insecure Code Generation
risks.description: Agent generates code with SQL injection, XSS, or command injection vulnerabilities
risks.mitigation: Vex scans all generated code with multiple security analyzers before execution

risks.name: Credential Leakage
risks.description: Agent accidentally includes API keys, passwords, or secrets in generated code or logs
risks.mitigation: Vex performs regex-based secret scanning on all outputs and memory states

risks.name: Malicious Dependencies
risks.description: Agent suggests compromised or malicious pip packages during dependency resolution
risks.mitigation: Vex validates all dependencies against safety databases and license policies

risks.name: License Non-Compliance
risks.description: Generated solutions use proprietary code or violate open-source licenses
risks.mitigation: Vex checks license compatibility and flags copyleft violations

implementation.title: Basic Agno Coding Agent Setup
implementation.description: Create a foundational code-aware agent with repository tools and sandbox execution
implementation.code:
from agno.agent import Agent
from agno.tools.github import GitHubTools
from agno.tools.sandbox import PythonSandbox

agent = Agent(
    name="CodeAssistant",
    model="anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    tools=[
        GitHubTools(),
        PythonSandbox(),
    ],
    instructions="You are an expert Python developer. Analyze code, fix bugs, write tests. Always validate solutions.",
    show_tool_calls=True,
    add_base_tools=False,
)

result = agent.run("Fix this bug in main.py: [paste code]")

implementation.title: Repository Context Integration
implementation.description: Load full repository context for comprehensive code analysis and modification
implementation.code:
from agno.tools.files import FileTools
from pathlib import Path

repo_tools = FileTools(
    root=Path("/path/to/repo"),
    include=["*.py", "requirements.txt", "tests/**"],
    exclude=["__pycache__", "*.pyc", "venv"]
)

agent = Agent(
    name="RepoCoder",
    tools=[repo_tools, PythonSandbox()],
    instructions="You have full repo access. Understand architecture before suggesting changes."
)

result = agent.run("Refactor the user authentication module for better security")

implementation.title: Test-Driven Development Loop
implementation.description: Implement iterative debugging with automatic test validation
implementation.code:
from agno.tools.testing import PytestTools

agent = Agent(
    name="TDD-Agent",
    tools=[
        PythonSandbox(),
        PytestTools(test_dir="tests/")
    ],
    instructions="""Write code that passes ALL tests.
    1. Read failing tests
    2. Write minimal fix
    3. Run tests
    4. Repeat until 100% pass"""
)

result = agent.run("Make all tests pass: pytest tests/ -v")

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Code Review
implementation.description: Coordinate security, performance, and style reviewers for comprehensive validation
implementation.code:
from agno import Assistant

security_reviewer = Assistant(
    name="Security",
    instructions="Find ALL security vulnerabilities"
)
performance_reviewer = Assistant(
    name="Performance",
    instructions="Optimize code performance and resource usage"
)

agent = Agent(
    name="CodeReviewTeam",
    assistants=[security_reviewer, performance_reviewer],
    instructions="Get consensus from all reviewers before approving code"
)

result = agent.run("Review this PR for production readiness: [code diff]")

vexIntegration.title: Wrap agent.run() with Vex Verification
vexIntegration.description: Decorator ensures all agent outputs pass security and correctness checks

vexIntegration.title: Sandbox Execution Safety Verification
vexIntegration.description: Verify generated code before sandbox execution to prevent malicious payloads

vexIntegration.title: Dependency and License Compliance
vexIntegration.description: Scan all requirements and generated imports for safety and compliance
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify_requirements

import re

class CompliantAgent(Agent):
    def parse_requirements(self, text: str):
        reqs = re.findall(r'([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)(?:==?[^\s]+)?', text)
        
        # Vex validates ALL dependencies
        safety_report = verify_requirements(
            requirements=reqs,
            checks=[
                "malware_free",
                "license_compliant",
                "no_abandoned_packages"
            ]
        )
        
        if not safety_report.is_safe:
            raise ValueError(f"Unsafe deps: {safety_report.issues}")
        
        return reqs

agent = CompliantAgent(name="SafeDepsCoder")
